stories.title: D11 Last Day of School — Class of 2026 Begins Summer Break Today

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
D11's last day of school is today. Summer break starts for all Colorado Springs families.

PPLD Summer Adventure: June 2. YMCA Pikes Peak camps: May 27.

Class of 2026, you made it. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
D11's Class of 2026 is officially done.

Palmer. Coronado. Mitchell. Achieve. Tesla. Bijou. Odyssey.

The last bell rings today for all Colorado Springs District 11 students. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM MT

Today is the last day of school for all Colorado Springs District 11 students — and that means summer officially starts for approximately 26,000 families across our community.

It has been a full week of graduations. Palmer High School went first on May 19. Coronado and Mitchell followed on May 20. Then Achieve High, Tesla STEM High, Bijou School, and Odyssey Charter wrapped things up on May 21. The academic year for the Class of 2026 is complete. For elementary and middle school families, today's final bell is the starting gun for summer.

What is coming up next? A few reminders:

YMCA Pikes Peak summer camps begin next week (May 27) at three Colorado Springs locations: Briargate, First and Main, and Tri-Lakes. If you have not registered yet, visit ppymca.org.

PPLD Summer Adventure starts June 2 with the theme "Unearth a Story" — free, for all ages, at every Pikes Peak Library District branch. Registration is open now at ppld.org/summer.

Territory Days is happening this weekend (May 23-25) in Old Colorado City — free admission, 180-plus booths, and kids' activities. And the Broadmoor World Arena hosts three D49 graduation ceremonies tomorrow.

For D11 families, the 2026-27 school year resumes in late August. For today: celebrate.

What are your summer plans? Drop them in the comments.

---

stories.title: MeadowGrass Music Festival 2026 — Family Weekend in the Black Forest Starts Today

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
MeadowGrass Music Festival starts TODAY in the Black Forest.

Kids 12 and under get in free. Kid Zone: Giant Bubbles, crafts, hay wagon, Kid Yoga. 30 min from downtown COS.

May 22-24. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:00 PM MT

The 17th Annual MeadowGrass Music Festival is happening right now in the Black Forest — and if you have not been, this weekend might be your best chance to check it off the Colorado Springs family bucket list.

The festival runs Friday, May 22 through Sunday, May 24 at La Foret Conference and Retreat Center (6145 Shoup Road, Colorado Springs). It is about 30 minutes from downtown.

Here is what families need to know:

KIDS 12 AND UNDER: Free with a paid adult. This is the headline number for parents trying to manage a family-friendly event budget.

KID ZONE: The Faricy Boys Kid Zone is built for children. Giant Bubbles from Milibo Art Theater. Crafts from Concrete Couch. A hay wagon. Kid Yoga. Art activities. This is not a corner-of-the-festival afterthought — it is a full program.

CAMPING: Onsite camping is available in the Ponderosa Forest. If you want the full experience, there is also limited luxury lodging at Lapp Lodge (7 rooms, accommodates up to 5 per room).

MUSIC AND MORE: Live bands, local food and craft brews, yoga, hikes, and workshops round out the weekend. The Beer Festival runs Friday.

Tickets are available at eventbrite.com or at rockymountainhighway.org/meadowgrass/.

Are you heading to MeadowGrass this year? Who else is going?

---

stories.title: East COS Childcare Desert — Resources for Families Struggling to Find Care

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Nearly 3 children per 1 childcare spot in El Paso County.

East COS is hardest hit. Childcare costs ~$20,000/year.

Help: CCCAP subsidies (719-444-8178) or Pikes Peak Family Child Care Assoc. (719-475-8828). #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:00 PM MT

If you are a Colorado Springs parent who has spent weeks calling childcare providers only to hear "no openings," you are not alone — and the numbers explain why.

A KOAA investigation found that El Paso County has nearly three children aged zero to six competing for every one available childcare spot. The problem is most concentrated on the east side, including the southeast corridor and the Fountain-Fort Carson area. Full-time childcare in the region costs approximately $20,000 per year — an amount that is causing some families to leave the workforce entirely rather than pay it.

The good news: there are programs designed to help. Here is where to start.

CCCAP (Colorado Child Care Assistance Program): Provides childcare subsidies for low-income families. El Paso County contact: 719-444-8178.

Pikes Peak Region Family Child Care Association: Helps families find licensed childcare providers in El Paso County. Call 719-475-8828.

Colorado Shines: A free online tool to search and compare licensed early learning programs across Colorado. Search at coloradoshines.com.

Colorado Enterprise Fund: The Child Care Loan Fund helps childcare providers who cannot access traditional financing — which can indirectly increase available spots in underserved areas.

PPLD LibGuide: Pikes Peak Library District maintains a "Child Care and Early Learning" resource guide at research.ppld.org with additional local contacts.

Have you navigated the childcare system in east Colorado Springs? What worked for your family? Share below.

---

article.headline: Nearly 3 Children Per Spot: East COS Childcare Crisis and How to Find Help

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers (9,300 Units, Amazon and Walmart)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers — 9,300 units (Amazon + Walmart).

Fall and entrapment hazard. Stop use immediately.

Destroy product, email photos for refund: joyfuljourneysrecalls@myjoyfuljourneys.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 5:00 PM MT

If you purchased a Joyful Journeys baby lounger on Amazon or Walmart, please stop using it immediately.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) has recalled approximately 9,300 Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers due to serious fall and entrapment hazards. The loungers were sold online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from December 2023 through October 2025 for between $40 and $60.

THE PROBLEM: The sides of the loungers are too low to properly contain an infant, and the enclosed openings at the foot of the lounger are wider than what is allowed under the Mandatory Standard for Infant Sleep Products. The loungers also have no stand, creating a fall hazard on elevated surfaces.

The recalled loungers came in these styles: green avocado, blue dinosaur, blue lambs, blue zoo animals, green minky, neutral elephants, pink zoo animals, and white minky.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Stop using the lounger immediately.
2. Remove the foam pad and padded bumpers from the cloth cover.
3. Cut the cover, foam, and pad in half.
4. Email photos of the destroyed pieces to joyfuljourneysrecalls@myjoyfuljourneys.com to receive a full refund.

Do not attempt to sell or donate the recalled lounger. This is a safety issue that affects other families.

Share this post with anyone who may have purchased one of these loungers.

---

article.headline: CPSC Recall: 9,300 Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers — Stop Use If You Have One

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger Recall

---

research.facts:
Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger Recall
**Sources consulted:**
1. CPSC.gov — Official recall page: "Joyful Journeys Recalls Baby Loungers"
2. cwcolumbus.com — "Baby loungers sold on Amazon and at Wal-Mart recalled over safety concerns"
3. growingyourbaby.com — "9,300 Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers Recalled"
4. classactionlawsuithelp.com — Joyful Journeys Baby Lounger Recall class action info
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- ~9,300 units recalled — confirmed CPSC.gov, cwcolumbus.com, growingyourbaby.com (consistent across all three)
- Sold at Amazon.com and Walmart.com — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Date range: December 2023 through October 2025 — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Price: $40-$60 — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Hazard: sides too low (fall risk), foot openings too wide (entrapment) — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Violates Mandatory Standard for Infant Sleep Products — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Styles: green avocado, blue dinosaur, blue lambs, blue zoo animals, green minky, neutral elephants, pink zoo animals, white minky — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Remedy: cut cover/foam/pad in half, email photos — confirmed CPSC.gov
- Contact: joyfuljourneysrecalls@myjoyfuljourneys.com — confirmed CPSC.gov

stories.title: Memorial Day Weekend Final Guide — Territory Days Closes Monday, D49 Wraps Up, Summer Begins

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
COS Memorial Day weekend:

Sat: D49 graduations — Sand Creek 9 AM, Falcon 1 PM, Vista Ridge 5 PM (Broadmoor World Arena)
Sat–Mon: Territory Days, Old Colorado City — closes Mon May 25 at 6 PM

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 PM MT

Colorado Springs families, here is your complete guide to Memorial Day weekend — and the summer that follows.

THIS WEEKEND:

Territory Days runs through Monday, May 25 at its Colorado Avenue location in Old Colorado City (23rd to 27th Street). Saturday and Sunday hours are 10 AM to 7 PM. Monday (Memorial Day) is the final day: 10 AM to 6 PM. Admission is free. More than 180 booths, live music, a petting zoo, and a kids' play area.

D49 Graduation Saturday: All three ceremonies are at Broadmoor World Arena (3185 Venetucci Blvd):
- Sand Creek High School: 9:00 AM
- Falcon High School: 1:00 PM
- Vista Ridge High School: 5:00 PM

COMING UP NEXT:

May 27: YMCA Pikes Peak summer camps begin at Briargate, First and Main, and Tri-Lakes. Visit ppymca.org to register.

June 2: PPLD Summer Adventure begins ("Unearth a Story"). Free, all ages, all branches. Register at ppld.org/summer.

For D11 families, this week marks the first week of summer break. The 2026-27 school year begins in late August.

What is your family doing this Memorial Day weekend? Let us know below.

---

article.headline: COS Memorial Day Weekend Wrap-Up: Territory Days Final Day, D49 Graduation, and Summer Begins
